sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाचएवं सततयुक्ता ये भक्तास्त्वां पर्युपासते । येचाप्यक्षरमव्यक्तं तेषां के योगवित्तमाः ॥
hindiTranslation: जो भक्त इस प्रकार निरन्तर आपमें लगे रहकर आपसगुण भगवान् की उपासना करते हैं और जो अविनाशी निराकारकी ही उपासना करते हैं, उनमेंसे उत्तम योगवेत्ता कौन हैं ॥
englishTranslation: Arjuna said: Those devotees who worship You with constant devotion, and those who worship the imperishable, unmanifest reality—who among them are the most accomplished in yoga?
essence: Devotion can face the divine as form or as formless reality.
speaker: Arjuna
simpleMeaning: Arjuna asks whether devoted worship of Krishna or worship of the imperishable, formless reality is the higher yoga.
simpleInsight: It is honest to ask which form of devotion leads deepest.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, with Arjuna still uncertain and the battle waiting, he asks Krishna a sharp question: is loving the personal divine better, or is worshipping the unmanifest absolute higher? Chapter 12 begins by comparing two ways of devotion.
modernRelevance: You stand between two paths: a relationship that feels alive, and a principle that feels pure. The question is not weak — it is the mind trying to find the deepest way to give itself.
translationLiteral: अर्जुन उवाच (Arjuna said) / एवम् (thus) / सततयुक्ताः (constantly devoted) / ये (those who) / भक्ताः (devotees) / त्वाम् (You) / पर्युपासते (worship) / ये (those who) / च (and) / अपि (also) / अक्षरम् (the imperishable) / अव्यक्तम् (the unmanifest) / तेषाम् (of them) / के (which) / योगवित्तमाः (most accomplished in yoga)
insight: It frames devotion as a real choice between personal love and abstract contemplation, preparing Krishna's answer.
